Step 343
Overview
Assessment is one of the vital parts of the teaching-learning process which is time-consuming and may cause exam takers to show up their potential if they are mis-assessed or mis-evaluated. Evaluation of Achievement (EA), on the other hand, is a program that focuses on evaluating progress in education and working hard to develop better ways to reach the deeper knowledge of exam taker’s understanding to provide helpful reports to exam takers, parents, instructors, institutions at different aspects of their purpose.
Evaluation of Achievement Standardized Exam (EASE) is an assessment that evaluates the achievement of an exam taker from the subjects he/she learned. The reports from the assessment demonstrate what exam takers have achieved and the progress they have made over time for their future learning requirements.
EASE provides assessments for English, Mathematics, Science, Biology, Chemistry, and Physics implementing International Curriculum such as Cambridge Checkpoint, IGCSE, and AS/A Level. Also, EASE provides assessments for National Curriculum subjects, including Religion and Moral Education, Civic Education (PPKn), and Social Science (IPS).
The EASE Cycle
Preparation
Getting ready for EASE exams ahead
Entries
Ensuring exam takers’ data in the system (Edunav and latihan.id)
Before The Exams
Preparing for the exam day: rooms, invigilators, and announcements
Exam Day
What to do during and immediately after each exam
Results and Reports
Results day, access to review, reports and certificates
Key Definitions & Responsibilities
Exam Center (EDUVERSAL)
Eduversal Exam Center is an exam organizer that conducts the preparation, implementation, and evaluation of the EASE exam held by all Eduversal partner schools.
The Eduversal Exam Center consists of the Eduversal Exam Committee and the Eduversal Heads of Departments, who are responsible for providing materials and test reports.
Additionally, the Eduversal Exam Center also has an IT Team that is responsible for the exam platform used.
Eduversal Exam Committee
The Eduversal Exam Committee is the Eduversal team responsible for the entire process of administering the EASE exam.
The duties and responsibilities of the Eduversal Exam Committee are as follows:
Preparing Handbook for EASE exam administration
Preparing a timeline for EASE exam administration and socializing it to the Eduversal partner schools.
Coordinating with Eduversal HoD regarding the preparation of exam materials (Pointers, Questions, Answer Keys).
Coordinating with Eduversal HoD regarding readiness to integrate exam materials with the Latihan.id platform.
Communicating with the School Exam Committee regarding the preparation compliance for the EASE exam in schools.
Communicating with the school IT Support regarding preparation for the implementation of the EASE exam at the school.
Providing socialization related to the technical implementation of the EASE exam to the School Exam Committee and School IT Support.
Organizing make-up exams and coordinating with schools regarding the implementation of the make-up exams.
Eduversal Heads of Department (HoDs)
Eduversal Heads of Department consist of the heads of the department who are responsible for the overall contents or materials to be tested in EASE exams.
The duties and responsibilities of the Eduversal HoDs are as follows:
Coordinating with School Subject Leaders in preparing materials to be tested in the EASE exam.
Coordinating with School Subject Leaders in making and compiling questions with the answer keys and rubrics.
Proofreading and revising the questions that have been made on Latihan.id.
Compiling questions that have been finalized in the form of a question package and integrating them into Latihan.id.
Preparing exam pointers and distributing them to schools through School Subject Leaders.
Finalizing exam results by evaluating and revising the questions on Latihan.id
Compiling the questions that have been evaluated and revised into the EASE question bank.
Analyzing the exam results and communicating them with school subject leaders and teachers.
School Exam Committee
The School Exam Committee is a team assigned by the school management that is fully responsible for implementing EASE Exams.
The duties and responsibilities of the School Exam Committee are as follows:
Communicating with the Eduversal Exam Committee in preparing the EASE Exams
Making a schedule and seating plan for EASE exams at school
Coordinating with the School IT Support to ensure that every exam taker is registered to Latihan.id and ready to take the exam
Coordinating with school technicians to ensure that the internet network to be used is adequate to conduct the exam
Organizing and choosing teachers to become exam invigilators for each class; Each class can be supervised by at least one Invigilator
Regulating the room that will be used as the test venue and ensuring the conditions are appropriate for use
Coordinating with the Invigilators and ensuring the exams run smoothly.
Checking and ensuring that all Invigilators have filled out the exam register (Berita Acara), as well as follow up on the findings of certain cases
Informing invigilators, exam takers, and parents about exam regulations
Choosing a responsible person for IT support for supporting possible technical issues encountered by exam takers
Announcing exam-related information in the Announcement in Latihan.id dashboard
Reminding and ensuring all invigilators to standby before the exam begins
Ensuring all invigilators don’t have technical issues, if any report directly to School IT support
Taking action about Invigilators notes after the exam
Subject Leader (School Head of Department)
The subject leader is a teacher appointed by the school as the person in charge of a particular subject.
The duties and responsibilities of the subject leader are as follows:
Communicating with the teachers to prepare the exam takers for the examination
Assisting Eduversal HoD in preparing materials to be tested in the EASE exam
Communicating with Eduversal HoD in making and compiling questions with answer keys and rubrics
Assisting Eduversal HoD, if necessary, in proofreading the questions that have been made
Providing scoring (for Essay Questions) according to the rubric and integrating it into Latihan.id
Analyzing the exam results and communicating them with school subject teachers.
School IT Support
School IT support consists of people assigned by the school and is responsible for the technical aspects of administering the exam.
The duties and responsibilities of the School IT Support are as follows:
Coordinating with the Eduversal Exam Committee regarding the technical implementation of the EASE Exam
Coordinating with the Eduversal IT team regarding the use of Eduversal and Latihan.id platforms
Checking and making sure all exam takers are registered on the Latihan.id
Allowing and prohibiting access to exam takers to take exams in accordance with the policies provided by the school
Monitoring the course of the exam and coordinating with the Eduversal Exam Committee if there are any problems
Invigilators
Invigilators are teachers assigned by schools to supervise and assist the exam takers during the exams.
The duties and responsibilities of the Invigilators are as follows:
Checking exam taker attendance on the latihan.id platform according to the class and subject being tested
Supervising the examination and reporting extraordinary events (if any) by writing them in the exam register (Berita Acara)
Accessing control center in latihan.id to:
Get the session code
Check exam takers' attendance, and report absent exam takers to the exam committee or homeroom teachers
Allowing the exam takers to join using smartphone if they do not have laptops/PCs available during the exam day, based on the school exam committee’ decision
Communicating with exam takers through the Live Chat feature on latihan.id
Paying close attention to the exam taker's behavior by checking each exam taker’s picture frame by frame from the control center
Recording exam takers’ conduct misbehavior, such as:
Leaving the room without permission
Turning off the web camera
Allowing late exam taker to start the exam if:
He/she is able to explain the reason logically and show an emergency
He/she obtains permission from the school
Checking the exam takers' screens through Goguardian or similar websites if they are using Chromebook.
In case exam takers have to do the exam online from home
School exam committee needs to:
Gather video-conference links for all classes from assigned invigilators
Inform the exam takers about the link and the online exam regulations
Contact the exam taker's guardian and/or exam taker who hasn’t joined the exam
The Invigilator needs to:
Provide a video-conferencing link and share the information
Meeting links and passwords are generated for each grade and to be shared with the exam committee two days before the actual exam date
Provide video conferencing session 15 minutes before the exams to:
Read the online exam regulation
Make sure exam takers meet minimum system requirements (internet browser, webcam, devices, etc)
Check the exam takers' dress code and the exam takers’ environment.
Give session codes to exam takers
Guide exam takers to start the exam
Rules & Regulations
These Rules and Regulations are the agreements between the exam takers (students), the school exam committee, and Eduversal. They set forth important rules that must be followed related to taking EASE. Please read the following carefully.  Exam takers must agree to abide by such requirements.
During the EASE Exam
EASE exams are conducted online. The exam takers are required to access the exam platform, latihan.id, through their Edunav account.
The following gives you the information about the online exam system and cannot be ignored;
The exam will be recorded.
Make sure that exam takers enter the correct exam and exam password.
It is the exam taker's responsibility to check and enter the correct exam session. The title of the exam can be checked on the exam card.
The time limit allocated for each exam package is based on the subject.
The submitted answers can be revised during the exam time.
Exam takers can navigate from one question to other questions by clicking the question number in the question navigation bar.
Exam takers can be removed from the session if they continue to do exam misconduct after being given a warning during the exam. The exam takers will be treated in accordance with the school regulations.
Exam takers can see their exam report and review it from their home dashboard.
The exam report and review button will be activated each day or a maximum of a day (24 hours) after the scheduled exam times in the GMT+7 time zone.
In addition, the following will give you information about the online exam system and regulations.
Required & Prohibited Items
Exam takers are required to bring the following items to the EASE exams:
A computing device (PC Desktop, Laptop, Chromebook, or smartphone) with a Webcam, including a charging adapter.
Earphones, only for the listening section
Calculator, only for allowed subjects.
Exam takers must not bring any of the prohibited items to the test.
Prohibited items include but are not limited to:
Smartwatches, fitness trackers, or other wearable technology (simple digital non-smartwatches or analog watches are acceptable)
Cameras or any other photographic equipment
Books or references of any kind
Papers of any kind; the scratch paper will be given by the invigilator when needed
Unacceptable calculators that have computer-style (QWERTY) keyboards
Additional items to bring during the exams:
Pen
Pencil
Transparent pencil case
Drinking bottle
If the exam takers do not bring the required items, or if they bring prohibited items, they may be denied from being admitted to or dismissed from the test site; in addition, the exam committee may decline to score the participant’s test, or cancel the score.
Minimum Device System Requirement
The computing device used by the exam takers needs to meet the following requirements:
PC Desktop, Laptop, Chromebook, or Smartphone (if the school allows)
The newest versions of Chrome web browser
Webcam
Stable internet connection (Wi-Fi or LAN)
Prohibited Behaviors
Exam takers may not engage in the prohibited behaviors set forth below:
Attempt to cheat or otherwise obtain an unfair advantage on the exams
Remove or attempt to remove any test questions or responses or essay topics or any notes from the testing room, including through memorization, give them to anyone else, or discuss them with anyone else through any means, including, but not limited to, email, text messages, or the internet
At any time, improperly access the test site, the test (or any part of the test), an answer key, or any information about the test
Refer to, look through, or work on a test section in the test site other than during the testing period for that test section
Attempt to give or receive assistance, including by copying or through the use of an answer key
Discuss or share information about the test including questions, answers, identifying information about the version or form of a test, or any other information that might compromise the security of the test at any time (including before the test, during the test, during breaks, or after the test)
Communicate with other exam takers or other individuals in any form while testing is in session
Allow anyone to see the test questions or your answers or attempt to see or copy others’ test questions or answers
Consult notes, other people, electronic devices, textbooks, or any other resources during the test or during breaks
Have subject-related information on clothing, shoes, or body
Use or access any prohibited items including devices or aids such as, but not limited to, mobile phones, smartwatches, fitness trackers, other oral or written communication devices or wearable technology, cameras, notes and reference books, etc., during or in connection with the test, including during breaks
Use a calculator on any test section, unless approved by The School Exam Committee as an accommodation
Leave the testing room without permission and prior to the conclusion of all sections of the test
Go to a locker or leave the building at any time during the test administration, including during breaks
Deliberately attempt to and/or take the test for someone else or attempt to have someone else impersonate you to take the test
Submit an essay that does not reflect an original and individual work
Provide false information to Eduversal or School Exam Committee
Disturb others
Consume food or drink in unauthorized areas
Exhibit or engage in confrontational, threatening, or unruly behavior, conduct, or communication toward or concerning any exam taker, test administrator, Invigilator, or exam committee
Allow an alarm or a personal item to sound in the testing room
Fail to follow any of the test administration rules set forth in these Regulations or in other registration information or directions given by the testing staff or rules of the test site
After the EASE Exam
Makeup Testing
The following policies apply to makeup testing:
Eduversal Exam Committee does not provide the makeup exams for EASE exams
Should there be students who cannot join the exams on the scheduled date(s), the school exam committee will arrange the makeup exams
Reports for the students who join makeup exams will not be submitted through Edunav system
Policies and Requirements
Eduversal may update its regulations and requirements from time to time, and they are subject to change up to 1 week prior to the test date. The exam takers are required to review these prior to each test administration.
Reports and Certificates
The exam results are released online through Latihan.id dashboard and school Edunav system.
Certificates are also given to the top 3 exam takers from each EASE subject.
How EASE is Scored
All multiple-choice questions are scored by giving points for each correct answer. No points are subtracted for incorrect answers or answers left blank. Hard questions count the same as easier questions.
The table shows all the scores counted in EASE (per subject):
For Primary Subjects
Subject
Type and Number of Questions
Scoring Details
Score Range
English
25 multiple-choice questions
4 points for each correct answer
0-100
Mathematics
25 multiple-choice questions
4 points for each correct answer
0-100
Science
25 multiple-choice questions
4 points for each correct answer
0-100
Religion and Moral Education
30 multiple-choice questions
3.33 points for each correct answer
0-100
Bahasa Indonesia
25 multiple-choice questions
4 points for each correct answer
0-100
For Secondary Subjects
Subject
Details
Score
Maximum Score
English
40 Multiple Choice (Reading, Grammar, and Listening) + 1 Writing
80% MC
20% Writing
0-100
Biology
35 Multiple Choice (MC) + 5 Free Response (FR)
70% MC
30% FR
0-100
Physics
35 Multiple Choice (MC) + 5 Free Response (FR)
70% MC
30% FR
0-100
Chemistry
35 Multiple Choice (MC) + 5 Free Response (FR)
70% MC
30% FR
0-100
Mathematics
35 Multiple Choice (MC) + 5 Free Response (FR)
70% MC
30% FR
0-100
Religion and Moral Education
40 Multiple Choice
2.5 points for each correct answer
0-100
Uses of EASE Reports
The results and the reports are the documents that are used to provide relevant information about exam takers’ progress to the exam takers, parents, support staff and other teachers.
There are different types of reports provided by the Eduversal EASE committee to serve different purposes.
The following are how EASE reports can be used by different school stakeholders.
School Management
Evaluating teaching and learning process
Identifying areas for school academic improvement
Showing parents what the school has to offer
Fairly evaluating and comparing the quality of teaching and learning across multiple schools
Helping for the preparation for inspections and accreditations
Supporting the decision-making process to prepare a better learning environment
Presenting the effectiveness of the teaching and learning process to parents
Providing the reports of students’ academic development effectively to all the school’s stakeholders
Providing extra evaluation of teacher observations
Subject Teacher
Supporting the identification of causes of underperformance and overperformance
Supporting the personalization of teaching and learning
Exam takers
Identifying their strengths and weaknesses
Encouraging them to take ownership of their own learning
Improving their motivation
Parents
Getting a clear information about the student’s progress in EASE subjects
Accessing student’s academic information easily through digital reports
Offering ways to support learning at home
Types of EASE Reports and Certificate
EASE reports are a collection of data, but very beneficial for teachers, school managers, exam takers, and parents.
After the exams are finished, the quick result can be found in the report section and reviewed on Latihan.id dashboard.
More comprehensive reports can be found on Edunav.  The reports can be accessed online through edunav.net > EASE > EASE Keys and Results.
The description of each type of report is as follows:
Individual Report
In this report, a question-by-question breakdown indicates where an exam taker has gaps in understanding, as well as the exam taker’s strengths and weaknesses. It also shows the comparison between the exam taker’s score with the class average, the school average, and the national average.
Class Report
This report identifies the high and low-achiever students, based on their scores on EASE exams. It indicates the class’s strengths and also shows topic-by-topic evaluation.
Validity Report
This report is to check the questions’ quality.
Topic Evaluation Report
This report can be used to identify understanding of specific topics compared to other Eduversal partner schools.
Certificate of Achievement
This certificate is given to the top 3 exam takers from each EASE subject. Its purpose is to contribute to the students’ motivation.
Appendix 1: EASE Checklist
Schools with their exam committee need to ensure the exam takers have been registered in the school’s own Edunav system for taking EASE exams.
The following is the work checklist for each role before the exam, during the exam, and after the exam for EASE.
Before exam
Exam committee
Finalizing exam takers list
Informing the exam schedule
Assigning invigilators for each class
School IT Support
Standing by on the exam days
Getting connected to Eduversal Exam Committee
Deactivating exam takers with financial problem
Invigilators
Opening an online meeting with exam takers 15 minutes before simulation
Reminding the absent exam takers
Guiding exam takers until they can start the exam
Exam takers
Coming to the classroom 15 minutes before the actual exam starts.
Sitting at a clean desk or table.
The desk or walls around must not have any writing.
The following items must not be on the desk or used during the exam, unless posted rules for the exam specifically permit these materials:
Calculator
Ruler
Protractor
The computer used to take the exam must not have more than one display or monitor. For example, if the laptop has another monitor connected, disconnect that monitor and use only the laptop screen.
Close all other programs or windows on the computer before beginning the exam.
The following tools are not allowed to be used:
Programs such as Calculator, Excel, Word, or PowerPoint
Communication programs, such as Skype
Any website page other than the exam window
Exam day
Exam committee
Reminding Invigilators to be on time
Ensuring each class has Invigilators assigned
School IT Support
Assisting invigilators and exam takers regarding technical problems
Reporting to Eduversal Exam Committee if necessary
Invigilators
Checking exam takers activities during exam
Filling out and submit Exam register through Latihan.id
After Exam
School Exam committee
Giving report access to the subject teacher in Latihan.id for checking exam takers’ answers and scores
Reminding subject teachers to check the exam result and create an analysis report
Making action plans according to the exam results
Appendix 2: The Online Exam Tutorials
Exam takers' tutorials (Step-by-step)
Step 1
Login to your Edunav account with the provided Username and Password. If you haven't received it yet, please contact the school admin as soon as possible.
Step 2
After successfully signing into Edunav, there is a “Go to Latihan.id” bar on the right of the Dashboard page. Click “Go to Latihan.id” and it will direct you to your Latihan.id page.
Step 3
On your latihan.id dashboard, go to Today’s Exam and you will see any online exams available on this day and click “enter”.
You will be asked to input the exam password; You can obtain the exam password from the exam Invigilator in the morning conference before the exam.
Step 4
After successfully entering the exam password, you will be directed to the exam instruction page.
Ensure you allow the system to use the web camera.
If you still have any problem with the web camera, follow the camera tips instructions on your page
Read the exam instructions carefully before starting the exam.
Click “Mulai” (Start) to start the exam.
Step 5
The following picture shows the exam page.
There are several things you need to know about the exam interface. The following are the detailed descriptions:
The Timer
Timer shows the countdown duration of your question.
Please be aware of the timer. When the countdown reaches zero, it will automatically submit and save your answer.
Camera Recording
Camera recording records your activity and behavior during the exam.
Live Chat
Exam takers can use the Live Chat to send a message to the invigilator, in case they need to get permission and/or have technical issues.
Question Number Navigation
Shows the current question number you are working on. You can jump to particular questions by clicking the number in the navigation bar.
Color indicator description:
Orange: Show your current question you are working on
Green: Question is answered
Black: Question is unanswered
Question box
It shows the exam questions.
Option box
Choose your correct answer.
Question Navigation
To continue to the next questions and back to the previous questions.
Select “Clear Answer” in the answer box to change your answer.
Step 6
After completing your exam, you will be directed to the exam summary page.
Step 7
You can see your exam score, report, and review from your home dashboard under the “Result” box. The Report and Review button will be activated at 01.00 PM (GMT+7).
Exam committee tutorials (Step-by-step)
Step 1
Login to your Edunav account with the provided Username and Password. If you haven't
received it yet, please contact the school admin as soon as possible.
Step 2
After successfully signing into Edunav, there is a “Go to Latihan.id” bar on the right of
the Dashboard page. Click “Go to Latihan.id” and it will direct you to your Latihan.id
page.
Step 3
Go to the “invigilatorship” menu to assign your teachers as exam Invigilators. To assign
the Invigilators, click the ‘ASSIGN PROCTORS’ button for the selected exam.
Write the assigned teachers as Invigilators and do not forget to click the save button to
save any changes.
To broadcast the video conference meeting link, click the “announcement
board” button. Write the message and click save.
The announcement will be posted in the exam taker dashboard; the following is an example:
To monitor the exams, click the control center for the selected exam.
Invigilators' tutorials (Step-by-step)
Step 1
Login to your Edunav account with the provided Username and Password. If you haven't
received it yet, please contact the school admin as soon as possible.
Step 2
After successfully signing into Edunav, there is a “Go to Latihan.id” bar on the right of
the Dashboard page. Click “Go to Latihan.id” and it will direct you to your latihan.id
page.
Step 3
You will see notification in the invigilator’s box if you are assigned as the exam
Invigilator. Proceed to the “control center” by clicking the control center button.
The following picture is the control center page.
The right box in the control center:
You can get the exam code and share the code with the exam takers;
You can get the information that helps you as the Invigilator regarding exam taker
status and troubleshooting to expand the information click the “Show information”.
exam taker statuses description
Troubleshooting
You can broadcast a message to all exam takers in the exam group by clicking the broadcast button in the message box.
You can record the exam note in the note tab and click save.
The left box in the control center contains three tabs:
Participant: The main element of the control center where the Invigilators can monitor the exam taker's behaviour, communicate with the exam taker, and record any exam misconduct during the exam.
Exam Questions: This tab helps the Invigilator to check the exam question in case any exam taker has a problem displaying the questions etc.
Summary: This tab summarizes the exam activities including the total number of exam takers, number of exam takers present, number of exam taker absences, and etc.
Note:
In any case, if exam takers have problems with the web camera or device’s compatibility and cannot start the exam, you can allow them to start the exam by first allowing the exam taker to start the exam without turning on the camera. In order to do so, from the control center in the exam taker box, click the “three dots icon” and select “Allow Access Without Camera”.
